name: Jaki Byard
abstract: John Arthur "Jaki" Byard (; June 15, 1922 – February 11, 1999) was an American jazz multi-instrumentalist, composer, and arranger. Mainly a pianist, he also played tenor and alto saxophones, among several other instruments.

concerts.label: Benefit Concert for the Student Nonviolent Coordinating Committee
Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Jaki Byard Trio
Midnight Performance: Jazz Stars at Midnight
Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Jaki Byard: Solo Piano

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: I got to know Jaki Byard very well... Jaki asked me if I wanted to go on Maynard’s band... So I said sure. So I jumped on the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: my first big teacher and head of the department was Jaki Byard... I brought in all these... Jaki Byard was able to do that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: Playing with Jaki was like playing with piano history. It was interesting to be on the bandstand
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: It was amazing playing with Jaki and sometime Jaki would come in and play saxophone
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: And then I had the maestro, Jaki Byard, there, who was like a dream to have at a university. So he went and studied with Hotep for about a year and a half, two years, and then he went and started studying his junior and senior year with Jaki Byard.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: The piano player in the house – the house intermission piano player was named Jaki Byard... When we put Jaki Byard on the cover, it didn’t do much for Jaki. I thought maybe it would, but it didn’t.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dan Morgenstern — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
